text.title: Go Concurrency Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Go Concurrency Patterns application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Worker pools have a fixed maximum size (`semaphore.Weighted`, `errgroup.SetLimit`, or buffered channel). No code path spawns goroutines without a bound.
rule.rationale: Unbounded `go func()` spawns under load saturate scheduler + memory; bounded pools provide backpressure naturally.

rule.statement: Every long-running goroutine takes a context.Context as its first argument and respects ctx.Done(). No goroutine runs forever without a cancellation source.
rule.rationale: Goroutines without context cannot be shut down; in HTTP handlers, this manifests as request-bound goroutines outliving the request.

rule.statement: Channels are closed only by the sender (or last sender via sync.Once). Receivers never close. No 'close from outside' for cleanup.
rule.rationale: Closing a channel from the receiver side panics the sender; this rule is the only safe convention.

rule.statement: Every spawned goroutine has a deterministic exit path: ctx.Done(), input channel drained, or wg.Done()-tracked completion. 'Eventually it returns' is not acceptable.
rule.rationale: Hand-wavy exit paths are how leaks happen; explicit signals make lifecycles reviewable.

rule.statement: Pipelines with N stages use the fan-out/fan-in pattern: bounded pool per stage, channels between stages, single output channel. Custom pipeline shapes require an ADR.
rule.rationale: Fan-out/fan-in is the canonical shape; bespoke shapes accrue maintenance cost and obscure backpressure.

rule.statement: Multi-worker operations use errgroup.WithContext so the first error cancels remaining workers. Bare goroutines with manual error channels are forbidden.
rule.rationale: errgroup handles cancellation propagation correctly; manual implementations consistently miss the edge cases.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
